description: Opt-in PreToolUse gate. On `git commit`, scans the staged diff for high-signal secret patterns (private keys, AWS/GitHub/Slack/Google/Stripe tokens) and blocks the commit if any are found. Read-only — it never prints the secret value, only that one was detected. Copy the `hooks` block into .claude/settings.json to activate.

hooks.command: in=$(cat); printf '%s' "$in" | grep -Eq 'git[[:space:]]+commit' || exit 0; git diff --cached 2>/dev/null | grep -Eq 'BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}|xox[bapr]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{30,}|sk_live_[0-9A-Za-z]{20,}' && { echo '[secret-scan] A possible secret was found in the staged diff. Commit blocked. Remove it, rotate it if it was real, and use an env var or secret manager.' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0
